description.content: A safety case template for autonomous vehicle deployment based on Positive Trust Balance

expression.value: UL 4600 - Standard for Evaluation of Autonomous Products
ISO 21448 - Safety of the Intended Functionality (SOTIF)
ISO/PAS 8800 - Road vehicles Safety and AI
German Ethics Commission Report on Automated Driving

description.content: Description of vehicle, operations, and stakeholder requirements
Relevant Safety Management System claims
Our safety advisory board includes a full set of stakeholder responsibilities
Results of independent assessment - Transparency assessment results
Results of independent assessment - Assessor independence verification
Results of independent assessment - Safety advisory board approval
Results of independent assessment - Regulatory compliance
Results of independent assessment - Ethics guidelines compliance
Results of independent assessment - 14 CFR Part 5 compliance

argumentPackage.name: Open Autonomy Safety Case - Main Argument

claim.name: Goal 1
claim.content: The autonomous vehicle is safe enough to deploy.

description.content: Top-level safety claim for the autonomous vehicle deployment

claim.name: Goal 3
claim.content: The organization is trustworthy.

description.content: Organizational trustworthiness claim - "Live It Right"

claim.name: Goal 4
claim.content: The vehicle is safe to operate.

description.content: Vehicle safety claim - "Build It Right"

claim.name: Goal 5
claim.content: We operate the vehicle safely.

description.content: Operational safety claim - "Operate It Right"
The argument strategy based on achieving a positive trust balance through organizational trustworthiness, vehicle safety, and safe operations
Description of vehicle, operations, and stakeholder requirements
The top-level claim is supported by organizational trustworthiness, vehicle safety, and operational safety through positive trust balance
Vehicle and operations context informs the positive trust balance strategy

claim.name: Goal 8
claim.content: We regularly share results of independent safety assessments with stakeholders.

description.content: Transparency through sharing assessment results

claim.name: Goal 9
claim.content: Assessor independence satisfies requirements of UL 4600.

description.content: Independent assessor qualification per UL 4600

claim.name: Goal 10
claim.content: SMS safety policies are aligned with stakeholder requirements.

description.content: Safety Management System policies meet stakeholder needs

claim.name: Goal 11
claim.content: SMS processes are in use.

description.content: Safety Management System processes are actively implemented

claim.name: Goal 12
claim.content: Our safety case is monitored & improved.

description.content: Continuous monitoring and improvement of the safety case

claim.name: Goal 21
claim.content: SRCs are approved by our safety advisory board.

description.content: Safety-related claims approved by safety advisory board

claim.name: Goal 22
claim.content: Our SRCs satisfy regulations in our operational area.

description.content: Safety-related claims satisfy applicable regulations

claim.name: Goal 23
claim.content: Our SRCs satisfy report guidelines.

description.content: Safety-related claims satisfy German Ethics Commission guidelines

claim.name: Goal 24
claim.content: Our SMS conforms with 14 CFR part 5.

description.content: Safety Management System conformance with aviation SMS standards

claim.name: Goal 25
claim.content: Our safety case is continuously improved in conformance with relevant sections of UL 4600.

description.content: Continuous improvement per UL 4600 requirements

claim.name: Goal 26
claim.content: Our organization conforms with IATF 16949.

description.content: Automotive quality management system conformance
Argument that transparency through sharing assessments builds stakeholder trust
ある具面と

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 7
argumentReasoning.content: An SMS is a best practice for managing system safety.

description.content: Argument that a Safety Management System represents best practice
Argument based on regulatory compliance

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 17
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to German Ethics Commission report.

description.content: Argument based on ethical guidelines for automated driving
Argument based on aviation safety management standards
Argument based on autonomous products evaluation standard
Argument based on automotive quality management standard
Our safety advisory board includes a full set of stakeholder responsibilities
Results of independent assessment - Transparency
Results of independent assessment - Assessor independence
Results of independent assessment - Safety advisory board
Results of independent assessment - Regulatory compliance
Results of independent assessment - Ethics guidelines
Results of independent assessment - 14 CFR Part 5
Results of independent assessment - UL 4600 improvement
Results of independent assessment - IATF 16949
Transparency and assessor independence support organizational trustworthiness
SMS policies, processes, and monitoring support organizational trustworthiness
Safety advisory board approval supports SMS policy alignment
Regulatory compliance supports SMS policy alignment
Ethics guidelines compliance supports SMS policy alignment
14 CFR Part 5 conformance supports SMS processes
UL 4600 continuous improvement supports safety case monitoring
IATF 16949 conformance supports safety case monitoring
Safety advisory board context for SRC approval
Assessment results support transparency claim
Assessment results support assessor independence claim
Assessment results support safety advisory board approval claim
Assessment results support regulatory compliance claim
Assessment results support ethics guidelines compliance claim
Assessment results support 14 CFR Part 5 conformance claim
Assessment results support UL 4600 continuous improvement claim
Assessment results support IATF 16949 conformance claim

claim.name: Goal 37
claim.content: We have analyzed all hazards posed by the vehicle.

description.content: Comprehensive hazard analysis completion

claim.name: Goal 38
claim.content: The vehicle conforms with vehicle safety standards.

description.content: Conformance with vehicle safety standards

claim.name: Goal 39
claim.content: The vehicle conforms with functional safety standards.

description.content: Conformance with functional safety standards

claim.name: Goal 40
claim.content: The vehicle conforms with AI engineering rigor standards.

description.content: Conformance with AI/ML engineering standards

claim.name: Goal 41
claim.content: The vehicle's intended functions are safe.

description.content: Safety of intended functionality (SOTIF)

claim.name: Goal 42
claim.content: The vehicle conforms with security standards.

description.content: Conformance with cybersecurity standards

claim.name: Goal 43
claim.content: We follow configuration management standards.

description.content: Configuration management practices

claim.name: Goal 44
claim.content: The vehicle conforms with data recording best practices.

description.content: Forensic data recording capabilities

claim.name: Goal 53
claim.content: We completed a hazard analysis according to this standard.

description.content: Hazard analysis per SAE J3307 STPA

claim.name: Goal 54
claim.content: Our vehicle conforms with FMVSS.

description.content: Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards conformance

claim.name: Goal 55
claim.content: We developed our system according to ISO 26262.

description.content: Functional safety development per ISO 26262

claim.name: Goal 56
claim.content: We developed our system according to ISO/PAS 8800.

description.content: AI safety development per ISO/PAS 8800

claim.name: Goal 57
claim.content: We developed our system according to ISO 21448.

description.content: SOTIF development per ISO 21448

claim.name: Goal 58
claim.content: We developed our system according to ISO 21434.

description.content: Cybersecurity engineering per ISO 21434

claim.name: Goal 59
claim.content: We manage these risks according to ISO 10007.

description.content: Configuration management per ISO 10007

claim.name: Goal 60
claim.content: Our system and operations conform with all recommendations in SAE AVSC 00004.

description.content: Data recording per SAE AVSC 00004

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 33
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to safety hazard analysis standards.

description.content: Argument based on hazard analysis methodologies

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 34
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to safety risk mitigation standards.

description.content: Argument based on risk mitigation through standards conformance
Argument based on configuration management standards

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 36
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to forensic data recording best practices.

description.content: Argument based on data recording for incident investigation

Argument based on Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards

Hazard analysis supports vehicle safety
Standards conformance supports vehicle safety through risk mitigation
Configuration management supports vehicle safety
Data recording supports vehicle safety through incident investigation capability
STPA hazard analysis supports hazard identification claim
FMVSS conformance supports vehicle safety standards claim
ISO 26262 development supports functional safety claim
ISO/PAS 8800 development supports AI engineering rigor claim
ISO 21448 development supports intended functions safety claim
ISO 21434 development supports security standards claim
ISO 10007 risk management supports configuration management claim
SAE AVSC 00004 conformance supports data recording claim
Assessment results support STPA hazard analysis claim
Assessment results support FMVSS conformance claim
Assessment results support ISO 26262 development claim
Assessment results support ISO/PAS 8800 development claim
Assessment results support ISO 21448 development claim
Assessment results support ISO 21434 development claim
Assessment results support ISO 10007 risk management claim
Assessment results support SAE AVSC 00004 conformance claim

claim.name: Goal 72
claim.content: Our operations support trip interruptions IAW all recommendations in SAE AVSC 00003.

description.content: Trip interruption operations per SAE AVSC 00003

claim.name: Goal 73
claim.content: Our post-crash operations conform with all recommendations in SAE AVSC-I-05.

description.content: Post-crash operations per SAE AVSC-I-05

claim.name: Goal 74
claim.content: Our remote operations conform with all recommendations in SAE AVSC-I-04.

description.content: Remote operations per SAE AVSC-I-04

claim.name: Goal 75
claim.content: Our first responder interactions conform with all recommendations in SAE AVSC-I-01.

description.content: First responder interaction procedures per SAE AVSC-I-01

claim.name: Goal 76
claim.content: Our safety inspections conform with all recommendations in SAE AVSC-D-03.

description.content: Safety inspection procedures per SAE AVSC-D-03

claim.name: Goal 77
claim.content: We monitor metrics in operations IAW all recommendations in SAE AVSC 00006.

description.content: Operational metrics monitoring per SAE AVSC 00006

claim.name: Goal 78
claim.content: Our operating procedures mitigate all hazards without technical mitigations.

description.content: Procedural mitigations for residual hazards

argumentReasoning.name: Strategy 69
argumentReasoning.content: Appeal to all relevant operational safety best practices.

description.content: Argument based on operational safety best practices
Argument that operating procedures address residual hazards
Argument based on safety management effectiveness
Relevant SMS claims providing context for operational safety
